name: Nyx Extended
description: Nyx, the primordial goddess of the night, is a powerful and enigmatic figure in Greek mythology. Born from Chaos, the void that existed before creation, Nyx embodies the very essence of darkness and the unknown. She is not merely the absence of light, but a potent force capable of inspiring both fear and awe. Nyx resides in the depths of Tartarus or beyond the Pillars of Atlas, where the sun never shines. She is depicted as a veiled woman, often winged, driving a chariot across the sky, bringing forth the night. Her power extends to controlling the shadows and influencing dreams. Nyx is the mother of numerous powerful beings, often fathered by Erebus (Darkness), including Hypnos (Sleep), Thanatos (Death), Moros (Doom), the Keres (Destinies), Momus (Blame), Oizys (Misery), the Hesperides (Evening Nymphs), the Moirai (Fates), and Nemesis (Retribution). While feared for her association with darkness and death, Nyx is also respected for her immense power and the crucial role she plays in the cosmic order. Even Zeus, the king of the gods, is said to have been wary of angering her. Her influence permeates the world, shaping the experiences of mortals and immortals alike. Nyx represents the mysteries hidden within the darkness, the potential for both comfort and terror that resides in the unknown, and the inevitable cycle of day and night that governs existence. She is the embodiment of the primal fear and wonder that darkness inspires, a force that existed before the gods themselves and will continue to exist long after they are gone. She is a powerful reminder of the limits of even the greatest beings and the enduring power of the primordial forces that shape the universe.
subtitle: Primordial Goddess of the Night

keyMyths.title: The Birth of Nyx
keyMyths.description: Nyx emerged directly from Chaos, the primordial void, making her one of the oldest beings in existence. Her birth marked the beginning of the separation of darkness from the initial nothingness.

keyMyths.title: Nyx and Zeus
keyMyths.description: Zeus, fearing Nyx's power, once refrained from pursuing Hypnos when the latter had lulled him to sleep. This demonstrates Nyx's influence even over the king of the gods.

keyMyths.title: Nyx's Abode
keyMyths.description: Nyx's home is located either deep within Tartarus or beyond the Pillars of Atlas, a place where the sun never shines and darkness reigns supreme. This location highlights her association with the furthest reaches of the world.

sources.author: Hesiod
sources.description: Provides the genealogy of the gods, including Nyx's birth from Chaos.
sources.text: Provides the genealogy of the gods, including Nyx's birth from Chaos.

sources.author: Homer
sources.description: Mentions Zeus's respect and fear of Nyx's power.
